welcome.title: PF2e × DSN Persistent Dice Bridge
welcome.body:
1. Open a PF2e roll dialog → task dice appear on the canvas.
  2. Drag-shake or right-click to throw. Ctrl+click multi-select then right-click throws them all at once.
  3. Empty slots fall back to RNG when you submit.

tray.noDice: No dice to roll for this formula.
tray.disabledBanner: Dice So Nice persistent dice are not active. Rolls will use RNG.

disabledReason.libWrapperMissing: Reason: the lib-wrapper module is not installed or not enabled. Without it, the tray fills but the value never reaches the actual roll — you'll see canvas value 11 but a different number in chat. Install/enable lib-wrapper.
disabledReason.moduleMissing: Reason: the dice-so-nice module is not installed or not enabled.
disabledReason.persistentOff: Reason: DSN's 'Display persistent dice' world setting is off (Settings → Module Settings → Dice So Nice).
disabledReason.interactivityOff: Reason: DSN's 'Allow players to interact with dice on the canvas' world setting is off (Settings → Module Settings → Dice So Nice).
disabledReason.managerMissing: Reason: DSN's persistent-dice subsystem hasn't initialized on this client. The DSN settings are 'requiresReload' — try refreshing the page (F5).

tray.secretBanner: Secret roll — you won't see the dice result. Falling back to system RNG.
tray.ceremonialBanner: Blind ritual — the ghost dice on canvas show "?" on every face. Throw them for the feel; the real result happens via system RNG on the GM's side.
tray.hint: Drag-shake or right-click the dice to throw. Ctrl+click multi-select then right-click throws all.
tray.rngAllTooltip: Reset all unlocked slots and let PF2e roll them randomly
tray.selectAllTooltip: Select every task die for this roll so you can throw them all in one drag (equivalent to Ctrl+clicking each).
tray.lockAccessTooltip: Re-lock — other players cannot drag the task dice for this roll
tray.unlockAccessTooltip: Unlock — let any player drag the task dice for this roll (this dialog only; next dialog re-locks automatically)
tray.perfTooltip: Cycle DSN image quality preset (Low → Medium → High). Updates DSN's per-user settings; reload (F5) to apply.

enabled.name: Enable bridge
enabled.hint: Master toggle for the PF2e × DSN persistent dice bridge.

requireAllSlots.name: Require all slots filled
requireAllSlots.hint: When enabled, the dialog falls back to pure RNG if any slot is empty at submit time. Default off — partial submission uses persistent dice for filled slots and RNG for the rest.

consumeAnyOwner.name: Consume any player's dice
consumeAnyOwner.hint: When enabled, your dialog can match persistent dice rolled by any user (cooperative play). Default off — only your own thrown dice fill your slots.

autoSpawnDice.name: Auto-spawn task dice
autoSpawnDice.hint: Default on. When a PF2e check / damage dialog opens, automatically spawn the needed persistent dice (e.g. 1× d20, 3× d6) onto the canvas. They are cleaned up when the dialog closes. Disable to spawn dice manually.

onlyConsumeOwned.name: Only consume module-spawned dice
onlyConsumeOwned.hint: Default on. Only persistent dice that the module spawned itself ('task dice') feed into roll dialog slots. Dice you spawn manually are free decoration and never get pulled into a roll. Disable to let any persistent die feed any open dialog.

hideDecorativeDuringDialog.name: Hide decorative dice during dialog
hideDecorativeDuringDialog.hint: Default on. While a check / damage dialog is open, your manually-spawned decorative dice are visually hidden from the canvas, leaving only the task dice you need to throw. They reappear when the dialog closes.

taskDiceLockedByDefault.name: Lock task dice to dialog opener by default
taskDiceLockedByDefault.hint: Default on. Each task die spawned for a roll dialog is locked to the player who opened the dialog — other players cannot drag, Ctrl+click, or throw it. Each dialog re-locks fresh; you can manually unlock for the current dialog via the 'Mine only' button at the top of the tray. Disable to keep task dice freely interactive for everyone.

autoSelectAllOnSpawn.name: Auto-select all dice on spawn
autoSelectAllOnSpawn.hint: Default off. When on, every task die spawned for a dialog is auto-selected (same as clicking 'Select all'). You can then throw them all in a single drag instead of Ctrl+clicking each individually. Especially useful for multi-die damage rolls.

respectSecretRolls.name: Respect secret rolls
respectSecretRolls.hint: Default on. When a check / damage dialog uses GM Roll / Blind Roll / Self Roll, the module treats it as a secret roll: task dice are only spawned on the client that should see the result, and never broadcast across socket. Other players see no dice on their canvas, preserving the secrecy. Disable to treat every dialog as public (players will see GM's secret throw animation, leaking the result).

suppressDsnThrowMessage.name: Suppress standalone DSN throw message
suppressDsnThrowMessage.hint: Default on. While a check / damage dialog is open, the standalone 'You rolled X' chat message DSN normally posts for a persistent throw is redundant — the user already saw the dice physically roll, and the PF2e check result message contains the value. With this on, that standalone message is suppressed; the PF2e check message is the only chat output. Standalone throws (no dialog open) are unaffected and keep their message.

settleBufferMs.name: Settle buffer (ms)
settleBufferMs.hint: Extra wait after `engine.rolling` flips false before we record the die value, to cover any visual lag between physics-done and render-done. Default 100ms. Range 0-1000ms.

matchPriority.name: Slot match priority
matchPriority.hint: How to choose which open dialog gets a freshly-thrown die when multiple match.

choices.byType: By die type only (first dialog wins)
choices.byOwnerThenType: Prefer dialog whose owner rolled the die

applyToReroll.name: Apply to rerolls
applyToReroll.hint: Use unconsumed persistent dice on the canvas as input for hero-point / fortune rerolls. Throw the new die first, then click reroll.

autoSubmitOnFill.name: Auto-submit when all slots filled
autoSubmitOnFill.hint: Default on. When every slot has a value from a thrown persistent die, the dialog auto-clicks the Roll button. Disable to keep manual control of submission timing (e.g. tweak modifiers after rolling).

autoSubmitDelayMs.name: Auto-submit delay (ms)
autoSubmitDelayMs.hint: How long to wait after the last slot is filled before auto-submitting. Default 100ms. Range 0-1000ms; raise to 500-1000 if it feels too snappy.

showLockButton.name: Show lock button
showLockButton.hint: Default off. Adds a lock/unlock button to each slot. A locked slot is preserved when 'Clear All' is used and won't be overwritten by newly-thrown dice. Most users don't need this.

suppressRedundantDsn.name: Suppress redundant dice animation
suppressRedundantDsn.hint: Default on. Without this, PF2e re-asks DSN to show the same dice flying in again after submission, even though you already threw them physically on the canvas. Keep on to skip the duplicate animation.

verboseLogging.name: Verbose console logging
verboseLogging.hint: Default off. Turn on to print per-step traces to the browser console (matched dice, settle timings, evaluate-wrapper injection, suppression-hook decisions). Useful for debugging or filing a bug report. Warnings and errors are always shown regardless of this setting.

rightClickAutoThrow.name: Right-click to throw
rightClickAutoThrow.hint: Default on. Right-click any of your own persistent dice on the canvas to throw it in a random direction at minimum velocity — no shaking required. The browser context menu over the dice canvas is suppressed when you have a die under the cursor.

mirrorThrowToHiddenViewers.name: Mirror task throws to hidden viewers
mirrorThrowToHiddenViewers.hint: Default on (world setting). When a player whose DSN visibility is 'Show only mine' or 'Hide all' would otherwise see nothing of another player's task-die throw, play a brief ephemeral 3D animation on their canvas with the predetermined result. Idle dice still don't appear — they only see the throw moment, then the dice vanish. Disable to keep hidden-viewer mode strict (no animation at all).
